articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- 82% of landlords are worried about the Renters’ Rights Act, with 44% expressing significant concern, leading to a reluctance to expand their property holdings.  
- 75% of landlords feel regulations are deterring portfolio growth, and nearly half (49%) are considering selling or downsizing their investments in the near future.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants, with a pressing deadline to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030.
- Rising energy costs, exacerbated by the Iran War, have intensified tenants' focus on energy efficiency and cost-saving measures in housing.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe that recent planning reforms will benefit their businesses.  
- Landbay reports a significant lack of confidence in the effectiveness of these reforms among property investors.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in properties come from outside, highlighting the need for landlords to maintain external pipework.  
- Experts warn that neglecting outdoor plumbing can lead to costly repairs, potentially running into thousands.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- Major changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales are looming with the Renters’ Rights Act, set to take effect on 1 May 2026, aiming to redefine landlord and tenant rights.  
- Landlords should prepare for increased requirements and regulatory oversight by following essential tips to navigate the upcoming transition smoothly.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act set to go live on May 1.
- With 58% facing a high risk of non-compliance, many property owners are urging for clearer guidelines.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting strategies to restructure portfolios and financing due to an easing in rental demand, moving away from the default of expansion.
- Recent ONS data highlights a slowdown in rental growth, emphasizing the importance of optimizing existing property portfolios.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- Rising competition from these buyers intensifies the challenges faced by those looking to purchase their first home.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the vital role of the private rented sector (PRS) with a focused strategy.  
- There is a growing concern about an exodus of landlords, highlighting the need for supportive policies to stabilize the market.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, HMRC mandates tax advisor registration for professionals like conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, aiming to enhance compliance.
- Non-compliance could result in severe penalties, with fines reaching up to £10,000 for those who fail to register.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that increasing licensing fees of £694 per property for landlords won't drive up rental prices.
- Landlords are expected to absorb the costs, with no financial relief from the council.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour’s Matthew Pennycook has explicitly stated that the party will not implement rent controls.  
- The announcement clarifies Labour's stance on housing policy amidst ongoing debates about affordability.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- Council aims to implement strict guidelines to regulate Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) and curb landlord excesses.
- New regulations intend to enhance tenant safety and community standards in HMO properties.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign launches Monday to educate tenants on fully utilizing their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- Tenants are encouraged to learn how to leverage this legislation for better rental agreements and protections.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The new Renters Rights Act is leading to a surge in Section 21 evictions, confirming warnings from expert Paul Shamplina.
- The legislation's impact is causing concern among renters and landlords alike as they navigate changing regulations.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing in the UK as landlords adjust ahead of the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to latest figures from Hamptons.
- The rising rental prices highlight the potential impact of looming regulatory changes on the rental market.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to be enacted in just 10 days, is expected to significantly impact the rental market by driving hobbyist landlords out of the business.
- This shift aims to enhance tenant protections but raises concerns about a potential reduction in available rental properties.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly venturing into property investment, navigating challenges such as strict regulations and rising taxes.
- This trend reflects a shift in investment strategies among younger generations, seeking stable returns in a fluctuating market.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing challenges in the current market, leading to price cuts, failed sales, and withdrawals, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.
- The tough conditions highlight increasing difficulties for property sellers as demand wanes.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is on the rise, it has been revealed.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is increasing its scrutiny of property valuations, leading to a higher number of cases being referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This heightened oversight suggests a potential crackdown on property valuation practices across the UK.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.

articles.ai_summary:

- The unusual incident highlights potential concerns about tenant privacy and property marketing practices in the UK real estate market.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Conveyancing Task Force labels referral fees as a "hidden tax," claiming they act as an unseen surcharge on homebuyers.  
- This revelation highlights the need for increased transparency in property transactions to protect consumers from unexpected costs.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.

articles.ai_summary:

- David Alexander emphasizes the strategic growth of Lomond’s operations following this expansion.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.

articles.ai_summary:

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices

Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.

articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation is climbing, with fuel and transport sectors experiencing the most significant price increases due to Middle East conflict.
- Broader economic repercussions are anticipated, as price hikes are expected to extend beyond these sectors.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of newly constructed homes bypass the open sales market, as highlighted by Alto's CEO Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson.  
- This trend raises concerns about accessibility and transparency in the housing market.
